proved: The product toolbar exposes headings, a quote block, bold, italic, underline, undo, redo, and policy-block creation through the original Lexical editor.
One semantic move command is used by labeled toolbar controls and guarded Alt+Shift+Arrow keyboard commands.
Move direction availability is derived from the current selection and unavailable controls are disabled.
The document outline is derived from live Heading 1 and Heading 2 nodes rather than persisted as duplicate state.
Outline controls select the current heading, substitute an untitled label, and render an honest empty state.
Supported heading, paragraph, quote, Unicode, policy identity/status, and text-mark structures survive a JSON editor-state round trip.
Connected policy reordering and partitioned non-structural status-versus-text edits converge in the deterministic two-editor harness.

The workspace suite, complete frontend suite, production frontend build, 41-check structural audit, and packaged Tauri build pass under the bounded operation watchdog; the log confirms Compiling app v0.1.10.
notProved: Remote-safe structural editing: the explicit expected-failure fixture demonstrates that moving one policy block while a disconnected peer edits that same block can lose the edit in the current Lexical Yjs root representation.
Packaged pointer, keyboard, focus, screen-reader, narrow-layout, reduced-motion, or dark-theme interaction.
Drag-and-drop reordering; the current pointer interaction is the toolbar move control.
Formatting support for lists, links, code blocks, tables, images, paste sanitation, or a 100-page stress budget.
A release/updater build containing this development checkpoint.
